response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong technical breadth and a genuine commitment to code quality and testing, he falls critically short on the core requirements for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ production experience; his resume shows only ~6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015). More significantly, the role explicitly calls for a technical leadership position mentoring teams and shaping platform strategy, yet his background is consistently described as individual contributor roles (software developer, engineer) without evidence of formal technical leadership, team mentoring, or platform stewardship. His infrastructure experience is limited to customer-facing products rather than internal compute platforms.
strengths: Demonstrated mastery of software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, refactoring advocacy, and quality-focused coding practices across roles
Proven ability to build and ship at scale: handled millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby, top-10 iOS app at WatchUp, and multi-country platform at Optilyz
Technical versatility and self-directed learning: proficient across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains with multiple languages (TypeScript, D, Java, C#, Swift)
concerns: Critical C++ gap: only ~6 months of production C++ experience (2014–2015), far below the 8-year requirement; recent work is JavaScript/TypeScript-focused

No technical leadership experience: resume shows individual contributor titles only (Developer, Senior Developer, Staff Engineer); no evidence of mentoring teams, managing engineers, or leading platform organizations

No cloud compute platform experience: background is customer-facing product engineering; lacks evidence of designing or owning infrastructure platforms, compute services, or distributed system architecture at platform scale
